Ensemble Method for Classification of Crossovers

Author

jmontero

Published

November 2, 2024

Ensemble classification model for predicting CO regions from random genomic regions combining linear regression and random forest. Select the number of folds for cross-validation, number of iterations for bayesian optimization and criterium to choose the best parameters.

Setting up environment

Packages

commonly_used_packages = c("data.table", "dplyr", "ggplot2", "tidyr", "stringr")
new_packages = c(
  "tidymodels", # ML framework
  "finetune",   # Bayesian optimization
  "doFuture",   # Parallel backend
  "tune",       # Hyperparameter tuning
  "rpart",      # Decision tree engine
  "glmnet",     # Regularized regression
  "ranger",     # Random forest engine
  "parsnip",    # Unified modeling API
  "vip",        # Feature importance
  "stacks",     # Stacking models
  "broom",      # Tidy model outputs
  "knitr"       # Report generation
)
pkgs = c(commonly_used_packages, new_packages)

for (pkg in pkgs) {
  if (!suppressMessages(require(pkg, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE))) {
    install.packages(pkg, character.only = TRUE, repos = "https://cran.uni-muenster.de/", quiet = TRUE)
    require(pkg, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE)
  }
}

Arguments and Parameters

filename = as.character(params$filename)
classifier = "ensemble"
k = as.integer(params$k)
n_iter = as.integer(params$n_iter)
criterium = as.character(params$criterium)
ncores = as.integer(params$ncores)
execution_mode = as.character(params$execution_mode)
random_seed = as.integer(params$random_seed)
set.seed(random_seed)
outputname = paste0(filename, ".ensemble.", k, ".", n_iter, ".", criterium, ".", random_seed)

Importing Data

if (execution_mode == "linux") {
  feature_table = fread(paste0("/vol/agcpgl/jmontero/features/Preprocessing/", filename, ".bed"))
} else {
  feature_table = fread(paste0(filename, ".bed"))
}
if (execution_mode == "linux") {
  features_to_ban = fread("/vol/agcpgl/jmontero/features/Feature.tables/ban_these_features")$Long_name
  features_to_ban = features_to_ban[features_to_ban %in% colnames(feature_table)]
} else {
  features_to_ban = fread("ban_these_features")$Long_name
  features_to_ban = features_to_ban[features_to_ban %in% colnames(feature_table)]
}
feature_table = feature_table %>% select(-all_of(features_to_ban))

Pre-modelling

Data Editing

Apply required data classes. Targets must be factors and features numeric. Add homologous pair as grouping variable.

# Create a grouping Feature for homoeologous chromosomes
feature_table = feature_table %>%
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      str_detect(chr_id, "A01|C01") ~ "grp1",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A02|C02") ~ "grp2",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A03|C03") ~ "grp3",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A04|C04") ~ "grp4",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A05|C05") ~ "grp5",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A06|C06") ~ "grp6",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A07|C07") ~ "grp7",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A08|C08") ~ "grp8",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A09|C09") ~ "grp9",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A10") ~ "grp10",
      TRUE ~ "unknown" # Catch unexpected cases
    )
  )
# Remove coordinates to keep only target and features to
# feed the model
data = feature_table %>% select(-starts_with("chr_"))
# Convert to tibble for tidymodels compatibility
data = as_tibble(data)
# Extract the integer features
integer_columns = data %>% select(where(is.integer)) %>% colnames()
# Convert integer columns to double
data = data %>% dplyr::mutate(across(all_of(integer_columns), as.double))
# Convert target to factor
data = data %>% dplyr::mutate(Crossovers = factor(Crossovers, levels = c(1, 0)),
                              group = factor(group))

Train-Test Split and Cross-Validation

# Split data into 80% training and 20% testing
data_split = group_initial_split(data, prop = 0.8, group = group)
training_data = training(data_split)
testing_data = testing(data_split)
The following groups will be used for testing:  
[1] grp3
Levels: grp1 grp10 grp2 grp3 grp4 grp5 grp6 grp7 grp8 grp9

Modelling

Base Models (Linear Regression + Random Forest)

Creation of Cross-validation Folds
# Create 5-fold cross-validation on training data
cv_folds = group_vfold_cv(training_data, v = 5, group = group)
Applying 5-fold cross-validation on data. 
# Group 5-fold cross-validation 
# A tibble: 5 × 2
  splits              id       
  <list>              <chr>    
1 <split [5020/1556]> Resample1
2 <split [5152/1424]> Resample2
3 <split [5052/1524]> Resample3
4 <split [4952/1624]> Resample4
5 <split [6128/448]>  Resample5
Fold 1:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp4, grp5, grp7, grp8, grp9, grp10 
  Assessment Groups:  grp2, grp6 
Fold 2:
  Training Groups:  grp2, grp5, grp6, grp7, grp8, grp9, grp10 
  Assessment Groups:  grp1, grp4 
Fold 3:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp2, grp4, grp6, grp7, grp8, grp10 
  Assessment Groups:  grp5, grp9 
Fold 4:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp2, grp4, grp5, grp6, grp9, grp10 
  Assessment Groups:  grp7, grp8 
Fold 5:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp2, grp4, grp5, grp6, grp7, grp8, grp9 
  Assessment Groups:  grp10
Recipe and Scaling

Scale features by subtracting the mean and dividing by standard deviation. Use the formula \(Crossovers .vs. Features\).

# Scale and make recipe
recipe_obj = recipe(Crossovers ~ ., data = training_data) %>%
  update_role(group, new_role = "ID") %>% # Treat chr_id and group as IDs, not predictors
  step_normalize(all_predictors(), -all_outcomes())
# Prepare the recipe (calculates means and sds on training data)
data_prepped = prep(recipe_obj, training = training_data)
# Apply scaling to the training set
training_data = bake(data_prepped, new_data = NULL)
Model Specifications
# Random Forest with tunable parameters
rf_spec = rand_forest(mtry = tune(), trees = tune(), min_n = tune()) %>%
  set_engine("ranger") %>%
  set_mode("classification")
# Logistic Regression with tunable parameters
lr_spec = logistic_reg(penalty = tune(), mixture = tune()) %>%
  set_engine("glmnet") %>%
  set_mode("classification")
Workflow Setup
# Workflow for Random Forest
rf_workflow = workflow() %>%
  add_model(rf_spec) %>%
  add_recipe(recipe_obj)
# Workflow for Logistic Regression
lr_workflow = workflow() %>%
  add_model(lr_spec) %>%
  add_recipe(recipe_obj)
Parameter sets
# Random Forest Parameter Set
rf_param_set = parameters(
  finalize(mtry(), training_data),
  min_n(),
  trees()
)
# Logistic Regression Parameter Set
lr_param_set = parameters(
  penalty(),
  mixture()
)
Metrics set
metric_set = metric_set(roc_auc, accuracy, precision, recall)
Hyperparameter Tuning
control_bayes_opt = stacks::control_stack_bayes()
# Random Forest Tuning
tune_results_rf = tune_bayes(
  rf_workflow,
  resamples = cv_folds,
  param_info = rf_param_set,
  iter = n_iter,
  metrics = metric_set,
  control = control_bayes_opt
)
→ A | warning: 32 columns were requested but there were 31 predictors in the data. 31 will be used.
There were issues with some computations   A: x1
There were issues with some computations   A: x2
There were issues with some computations   A: x3
There were issues with some computations   A: x4
There were issues with some computations   A: x5
! No improvement for 10 iterations; returning current results.
There were issues with some computations   A: x5
There were issues with some computations   A: x5
# Logistic Regression Tuning
tune_results_lr = tune_bayes(
  lr_workflow,
  resamples = cv_folds,
  param_info = lr_param_set,
  iter = n_iter,
  metrics = metric_set,
  control = control_bayes_opt
)
! No improvement for 10 iterations; returning current results.

Model Stacking

# Finalize the random forest workflow with the best hyperparameters selected
rf_final_fit =
  finalize_workflow(
    rf_workflow,
    select_best(
      tune_results_rf, metric = criterium)
  ) %>%
  fit_resamples(
    resamples = cv_folds,
    control = control_bayes_opt
  )
# Finalize the logistic regression workflow
lr_final_fit =
  finalize_workflow(
    lr_workflow,
    select_best(
      tune_results_lr, metric = criterium
    )
  ) %>%
  fit_resamples(
    resamples = cv_folds,
    control = control_bayes_opt
  )
# Ensemble stack creation
ensemble_stack = stacks() %>%
  add_candidates(rf_final_fit) %>%
  add_candidates(lr_final_fit)
ensemble_model = ensemble_stack %>%
  blend_predictions() %>%
  fit_members()
print(ensemble_model)
── A stacked ensemble model ─────────────────────────────────────


Out of 2 possible candidate members, the ensemble retained 2.

Penalty: 1e-04.

Mixture: 1.


The 2 highest weighted member classes are:
# A tibble: 2 × 3
  member                   type         weight
  <chr>                    <chr>         <dbl>
1 .pred_0_lr_final_fit_1_1 logistic_reg   2.72
2 .pred_0_rf_final_fit_1_1 rand_forest    1.54

Post-modelling

Performance Metrics

Calculating Performance Metrics
# Obtain predictions with ensemble model
ensemble_predictions_factor = predict(ensemble_model, new_data = data)
ensemble_predictions_prob   = predict(ensemble_model, new_data = data, type = "prob")
# Calculate metrics
metrics_df = data.frame(truth = data$Crossovers, factor = ensemble_predictions_factor$.pred_class, prob = ensemble_predictions_prob$.pred_1)
accuracy_result = metrics_df %>% accuracy(truth = truth, estimate = factor)
precision_result = metrics_df %>% precision(truth = truth, estimate = factor)
recall_result = metrics_df %>% recall(truth = truth, estimate = factor)
roc_auc_result = metrics_df %>% roc_auc(truth = truth, prob)
# Combine results into a single data frame (optional)
results = bind_rows(
  accuracy_result,
  precision_result,
  recall_result,
  roc_auc_result
)
print(results)
# A tibble: 4 × 3
  .metric   .estimator .estimate
  <chr>     <chr>          <dbl>
1 accuracy  binary         0.735
2 precision binary         0.734
3 recall    binary         0.739
4 roc_auc   binary         0.843
Plotting Performance Metrics
# Prepare data for plotting
metrics_table = results %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Model = "ensemble") %>%
  dplyr::rename(Metric = .metric, Estimate = .estimate) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Std_Dev = NA) %>% # Placeholder for standard deviation
  dplyr::select(Model, Metric, Estimate, Std_Dev)
# Plot mean metrics
metrics_plot_cv =
  ggplot(metrics_table, aes(x = Metric, y = Estimate, fill = Metric)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Estimate - Std_Dev, ymax = Estimate + Std_Dev), width = 0.2) +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Average Performance of ", "ensemble", " on CV Set"),
    x = "Metric",
    y = "Estimate"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
plot(metrics_plot_cv)
ROC Curve
Random Forest
# ROC for Random Forest
# Collect predictions across all folds
cv_predictions_rf = tune_results_rf %>%
  collect_predictions()
# Get ROC curve data
roc_curve_data_rf = roc_curve(cv_predictions_rf,
                           truth = Crossovers, .pred_1)
# Plot the data
roc_curve_plot_rf =
  autoplot(roc_curve_data_rf) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)",
    y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 0.75, y = 0.25, label = paste("AUC =", round(roc_auc(cv_predictions_rf, truth = Crossovers, .pred_1)$.estimate, 3)),
    color = "blue", size = 5, hjust = 0
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1)#,
    #plot.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1)
  )
plot(roc_curve_plot_rf)
Logistic Regression
# ROC for Logistic Regression
# Collect predictions across all folds
cv_predictions_lr = tune_results_lr %>%
  collect_predictions()
# Get ROC curve data
roc_curve_data_lr = roc_curve(cv_predictions_lr,
                              truth = Crossovers, .pred_1)
# Plot the data
roc_curve_plot_lr =
  autoplot(roc_curve_data_lr) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)",
    y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 0.75, y = 0.25, label = paste("AUC =", round(roc_auc(cv_predictions_lr, truth = Crossovers, .pred_1)$.estimate, 3)),
    color = "blue", size = 5, hjust = 0
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1)#,
    #plot.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1)
  )
Warning: The `size` argument of `element_rect()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 3.4.0.
ℹ Please use the `linewidth` argument instead.
plot(roc_curve_plot_lr)
Ensemble Model
# ROC for Ensemble
ensemble_predictions = predict(ensemble_model, new_data = testing_data, type = "prob")
ensemble_predictions$Crossovers = testing_data$Crossovers
roc_auc_ensemble = roc_curve(ensemble_predictions, truth = Crossovers, .pred_1)
ensemble_plot = autoplot(roc_auc_ensemble) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Ensemble ROC Curve (AUC =", round(roc_auc(ensemble_predictions, truth = Crossovers, .pred_1)$.estimate, 3), ")"),
    x = "1 - Specificity",
    y = "Sensitivity"
  )
plot(ensemble_plot)
All Models Combined
# Calculate AUC values for each model
auc_lr = round(roc_auc(cv_predictions_lr, truth = Crossovers, .pred_1)$.estimate, 3)
auc_rf = round(roc_auc(cv_predictions_rf, truth = Crossovers, .pred_1)$.estimate, 3)
auc_ensemble = round(roc_auc(ensemble_predictions, truth = Crossovers, .pred_1)$.estimate, 3)

# Update labels with AUC values
legend_labels = c(
  paste0("Logistic Regression (AUC = ", auc_lr, ")"),
  paste0("Random Forest (AUC = ", auc_rf, ")"),
  paste0("Ensemble (AUC = ", auc_ensemble, ")")
)

# Combined ROC Plot with Legend in Bottom-Right Corner and No Legend Title
combined_roc_plot = ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = roc_curve_data_lr, aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity, color = "Logistic Regression")) +
  geom_line(data = roc_curve_data_rf, aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity, color = "Random Forest")) +
  geom_line(data = roc_auc_ensemble, aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity, color = "Ensemble")) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)",
    y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)",
    color = NULL  # This also removes the legend title but theme is preferred for finer control
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Logistic Regression" = "blue", 
               "Random Forest" = "red", 
               "Ensemble" = "green"),
    labels = legend_labels  # Use dynamic labels with AUC values
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1),
    legend.position = c(0.75, 0.25),  # Bottom-right corner inside the plot
    legend.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white"),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = NA),
    legend.title = element_blank()  # Removes the legend title
  )
Warning: A numeric `legend.position` argument in `theme()` was deprecated in ggplot2
3.5.0.
ℹ Please use the `legend.position.inside` argument of `theme()` instead.
# Display the plot
plot(combined_roc_plot)

Feature importance with Ensemble Model

Importance Calculation via Permutation
# Define a custom prediction wrapper for the ensemble
predict_ensemble = function(model, newdata) {
  predict(model, new_data = newdata, type = "prob")[[".pred_1"]]
}
# Calculate permutation importance
importance_data = vip::vi_permute(
  ensemble_model,
  train = testing_data[, -which(names(testing_data) == "Crossovers")],
  target = testing_data$Crossovers,
  metric = "roc_auc",
  smaller_is_better = FALSE,
  pred_wrapper = predict_ensemble,
  nsim = 300,
  event_level = "first"
)
Importance Direction
# Calculate mean feature values by group (CO vs random)
group_means = data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Crossovers) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    dplyr::across(where(is.numeric), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Crossovers, names_to = "Feature", values_to = "Mean_Value")
# Split means into separate tables for CO and random groups
means_CO = group_means %>% dplyr::filter(Crossovers == 1) %>% dplyr::select(-Crossovers)
means_random = group_means %>% dplyr::filter(Crossovers == 0) %>% dplyr::select(-Crossovers)
# Merge means and calculate importance direction
feature_directions = dplyr::left_join(
  means_CO, means_random,
  by = "Feature", suffix = c("_CO", "_random")
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Direction = case_when(
      Mean_Value_CO > Mean_Value_random ~ "positive",
      Mean_Value_CO < Mean_Value_random ~ "negative",
      TRUE ~ "neutral"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Feature, Direction)
# Merge direction with importance data
importance_data = importance_data %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Importance_Type = "Permutation-based Feature Importance") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(feature_directions, by = "Feature") %>%
  dplyr::rename(Feature_Name = Feature, Direction = Direction) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(Importance))
# Modify feature names to make them shorter
importance_data = importance_data %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Feature_Name =
      unlist(lapply(strsplit(gsub("_", " ", importance_data$Feature_Name), "\\."), function(x) { x[2] })))
Feature Importance Plotting
feature_importance_plot = 
  suppressWarnings(
    ggplot(importance_data, aes(x = Importance, y = reorder(Feature_Name, Importance), fill = Direction)) +
    # Create horizontal bars
    geom_col() +
    # Add labels at the end of each bar
    geom_text(aes(label = Feature_Name),
              hjust = -0.2,                # Position labels slightly outside the bar
              size = 2,                    # Adjust label size
              color = "black") +
    # Custom fill colors for bar direction
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("positive" = "red", "negative" = "blue")) +
    # Adjust plot labels and appearance
    labs(
      title = "", #paste0("Feature Importance obtained with ", classifier_print),
      x = "Permutation-based Importance",
      y = ""  # Remove y-axis label
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_blank(),               # Remove y-axis text
      axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),             # Remove y-axis ticks
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black"), # White background, black border
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),     # Center-align plot title
      legend.position = "none",                    # Remove legend
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    expand_limits(x = max(importance_data$Importance) * 1.1)  # Add space for labels
  )
plot(feature_importance_plot)
Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_text()`).
